questions.question: What is the difference between abstract class and interface?
questions.explanation: Abstract class: can have both abstract and concrete methods, state, constructors. Interface: only abstract methods (until Java 8), no state, no constructors. A class can implement multiple interfaces but extend only one abstract class. Use interfaces for contracts, abstract classes for shared implementation.

questions.question: Explain garbage collection in Java.
questions.explanation: Java automatically manages memory through garbage collection. When objects are no longer reachable, the GC reclaims their memory. Algorithms include mark-and-sweep, copying, generational collection. Developers shouldn't rely on explicit garbage collection (System.gc() is just a suggestion).

questions.question: What are Java generics and why are they useful?
questions.explanation: Generics provide compile-time type safety for collections and other data structures. Enable writing generic algorithms that work with different types. Reduce need for casting. Syntax: List<String> list = new ArrayList<>(). Type erasure removes generic information at runtime.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the volatile keyword?
questions.explanation: volatile ensures that reads and writes to a variable are atomic and visible across threads. Prevents compiler optimizations that might cache the value. Doesn't provide mutual exclusion like synchronized. Used for flags and status variables.
